title: centaurea nigra subsp. carpetana
intro: Centaurea nigra subsp. carpetana, also known as the Cornflower or Mountain Cornflower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family, characterized by its blue or purple flowers and tall, slender stems. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds, sore throats, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Centaurea nigra subsp. carpetana include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and support the immune system, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for colds and flu. Centaurea nigra subsp. carpetana can be prepared in various ways, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
